headline: Non-hardware AI investments could exceed $1tn globally in the coming years: GS
summary: Investing.com -- Non-hardware investments in artificial intelligence could surpass $1 trillion globally in the coming years, Goldman Sachs said, as companies pour money into data infrastructure, software and organizational redesign to capture the technology's productivity benefits.

headline: Umicore - Transparency notification by The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
summary: Transparency notification byThe Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. In accordance with article 14, § 1 of the law of 2 May 2007 on the disclosure of major holdings, Umicore was recently notified by The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. that it has crossed the legal threshold of 5% for the direct voting rights and equivalent financial instruments upwards on 29 April 2026. The total holding of direct voting rights and equivalent financial instruments of The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. stands at 5.40% on 29 April 2026

The total holding of direct voting rights and equivalent financial instruments of The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. stands at 5.40% on 29 April 2026

headline: Bank Stock Buybacks Hit a Record in First Quarter. Citi, BofA, and Goldman Were Leaders.
summary: The country’s largest banks, flush with record earnings and capital, executed their largest quarterly stock repurchases ever in the first three months of the year.  The 21 large banks covered by Barclays analyst Jason Goldberg bought back $40 billion in the first quarter, up from $34 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025 and from the prior record of $38 billion in the 2019 fourth quarter, just before the Covid crisis.  The industry leaders, based on the biggest percentage reductions in share counts in the first quarter, were  M&T Bank  (3.9% reduction),  First Citizens Bancshares  (3.8%),  Citi  group (3.1%),  Bank of America (1.9%), and  Goldman Sachs Group  (1.8%).

content.title: Bank Stock Buybacks Hit a Record in First Quarter. Citi, BofA, and Goldman Were Leaders.

headline: Veteran analyst resets S&P 500 target for the rest of 2026
summary: Wall Street is getting a lot more comfortable with higher stock prices, but Ed Yardeni is moving the goalpost in a big way. According to a Fortune report, the veteran market watcher just raised his 2026 year-end S&P 500 forecast to 8,250 from 7,700. For perspective, that’s the most bullish call ...
